seo.title: Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents in Semantic Kernel Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix sensitive data leakage prevention in ai agents in Semantic Kernel agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Semantic Kernel agents, sensitive data leakage occurs when plugins, planners, or memory components inadvertently expose PII, API keys, or confidential information in function outputs, chat histories, or execution traces. This is exacerbated by plugin chaining where one plugin's output feeds into another, propagating sensitive data across the agent workflow. Without proper filtering, planners may generate execution plans that log or return unredacted sensitive content, leading to breaches in enterprise deployments.

howItManifests.symptom: User email from authentication plugin appears in error messages of downstream analysis plugin
howItManifests.impact: PII exposed in logs and traces across multiple plugin invocations

howItManifests.symptom: Planner includes raw API keys from memory in generated function invocation plans
howItManifests.impact: Execution plans containing credentials visible to developers and logged systems

howItManifests.symptom: Chat memory retrieves and exposes redacted conversation history containing credit card numbers
howItManifests.impact: Historical sensitive data reintroduced into current conversations

howItManifests.symptom: Customer data from CRM plugin contaminates output of unrelated reporting plugin
howItManifests.impact: Wrongful exposure of customer PII in business reports and analytics

detection.name: Plugin Output PII Scanner
detection.description: Scans Semantic Kernel function results for common PII patterns using regex before returning to user

detection.name: Memory Store Leak Detector
detection.description: Inspects Semantic Kernel memory embeddings and chat history for sensitive data before retrieval

detection.name: Planner Plan Leak Monitor
detection.description: Intercepts Semantic Kernel planner's generated plans to detect sensitive data in function parameters

fixes.name: PII Redaction Filter
fixes.description: Semantic Kernel function result filter that redacts PII before returning to user or logging

fixes.name: Secure Memory Store
fixes.description: Pre-processes chat history before storing in Semantic Kernel memory to remove sensitive data
fixes.code:
from semantic_kernel.memory import SemanticTextMemory
import semantic_kernel as sk

class SecureMemoryStore:
    def __init__(self, kernel: sk.Kernel):
        self.memory = SemanticTextMemory(storage=kernel.memory_store)
        self.redactor = PIIRedactor()

    async def store_message_securely(self, collection: str, message: str, user_id: str):
        # Redact before storing
        clean_message = self.redactor.redact(message)
        
        # Store metadata separately
        await self.memory.save_information(
            collection=collection,
            text=clean_message,
            id=f'{user_id}_{hash(message)}',
            description=f"Redacted chat for {user_id}",
            add_collection=True
        )

    async def retrieve_safe(self, collection: str, query: str, limit: int = 5):
        results = await self.memory.search_async(
            collection=collection,
            query=query,
            limit=limit
        )
        return [r.text for r in results]

# Usage
secure_memory = SecureMemoryStore(kernel)
await secure_memory.store_message_securely('chat_history', user_message, 'user123')

fixes.name: Safe Planner Wrapper
fixes.description: Wraps Semantic Kernel planner to sanitize function parameters before execution

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly as a Semantic Kernel filter on function results, automatically scanning all plugin outputs, planner plans, and memory retrievals for sensitive data patterns. Using the Vex SDK (pip install semantic-kernel vex-sdk), you add a single filter that detects PII, API keys, and credentials across your entire agent workflow without modifying existing plugins or planners.

Vex provides real-time remediation by redacting detected sensitive data and alerting on leakage patterns specific to your Semantic Kernel deployment. It automatically learns your data patterns and creates custom detectors for organization-specific sensitive information. The filter operates at the kernel level, catching leaks from plugin chaining, memory contamination, and planner outputs before they reach logs or users.

Integration takes <5 minutes and works across Python and TypeScript Semantic Kernel implementations. Vex maintains full audit trails and compliance reports, essential for enterprise deployments handling customer data.
vexAutomation.code:
import semantic_kernel as sk
from vex_sdk import VexKernelFilter

# Install: pip install semantic-kernel vex-sdk

kernel = sk.Kernel()

# Add Vex as kernel filter - catches ALL function results
vex_filter = VexKernelFilter(
    api_key="your-vex-api-key",
    detectors=[
        "pii",
        "api_keys", 
        "credentials",
        "financial_data"
    ],
    auto_redact=True,
    alert_webhooks=["https://your-slack.com"]
)

kernel.filters.add(vex_filter.filter_function)

# Your existing plugins work unchanged
kernel.import_plugin_from_directory("plugins/customer", "CustomerPlugin")

# Vex automatically protects:
# ✅ Plugin chaining
# ✅ Planner outputs
# ✅ Memory retrievals
# ✅ All function results

chat_service = kernel.get_chat_service()
response = await chat_service.get_chat_message_content(
    "Analyze customer profile",
    history=[]
)

# Vex already redacted any PII before this line executes
print(response.content)
